title: 5  Unweighted statistics
text:
5.2 Unweighted statistics functions
Functions are:
* unweighted_mean.
* unweighted_median
* unweighted_sum.
* unweighted_perc.
* unweighted_count.
Unweighted count - not optional; always calculated.


5.2.1 unweighted_mean

unweighted_mean <- function(
    data, # a data frame
    outcomes, # a character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, each element containing a character vector of predictor variables
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"; returns either standard error or confidence intervals 
    base = NULL
) {suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({
  
  output <- map(outcomes, function(cur_outcome) {
    
    base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = cur_outcome)) 
        
    # Remove missing data
    one_total_table <- data %>% 
      filter(!is.na(.data[[cur_outcome]])) %>% 
      summarise(estimate = mean(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
          n = n(), 
          sd = sd(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
          estimate_se = sd / sqrt(n), 
          estimate_low = estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se,
          estimate_upp = estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se, 
          base = sum(n)) %>% 
          select(-n, -sd) %>% 
        mutate("{cur_outcome}" := "mean") %>% 
        mutate(outcome = cur_outcome) %>% 
        rename(any_of(c(o_cat = cur_outcome))) %>% 
        mutate(o_lab = attributes(data[[cur_outcome]])$label) %>% 
        mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
  
    # Create cross-breaks
    if(!is.null(predictors)){
    predictor_tables <- map(predictors, function(cur_predictor) { 
      
            # Handle cases with and without predictor variables
        if (length(cur_predictor) > 0) {
          
          # Vector containing predictors and outcomes
          predictor_and_outcome <- c(cur_predictor, cur_outcome)
          base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = predictor_and_outcome)) 
          
          one_predictor_table <- data %>%
            # Filter missing values
            filter(rowSums(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome), ~ is.na(.))) == 0) %>%
            # Group data
            group_by(across(all_of(cur_predictor))) %>% 
            # Weighted mean + base sizes, with grouping vars
            summarise(estimate = mean(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
                      n = n(), 
                      sd = sd(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
                      estimate_se = sd / sqrt(n), 
                      estimate_low = estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se,
                      estimate_upp = estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se, 
                      base = sum(n)) %>% 
                      select(-n, -sd) %>% 
            # Added for consistency when merged with categorical outcome tables. 
            mutate("{cur_outcome}" := "mean") %>% 
            # Standardise names so can stack results across different predictors and outcomes. 
            standardise_names(data = data, preds = cur_predictor, out_var = cur_outcome) %>%
            mutate(cross_break = paste0(cur_predictor, collapse = "_X_")) %>%
            mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
        }
      
      return(one_predictor_table)
      
    })
    
    # Stack tables one on another
    predictor_tables <- bind_rows(predictor_tables)
    
    } # Predictors ends
    
    # Bind crossbreaks and frequency table together
    if(!is.null(predictors)){table_for_one_outcome <- bind_rows(one_total_table, predictor_tables)
    } else {table_for_one_outcome <- one_total_table}
    
    table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>%
      mutate(stat = "mean") %>%
      # Add 'Total' to predictor category variables for frequency section of the table.
      mutate(across(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), ~case_when(is.na(.x) ~ "Total", TRUE ~ as.character(.x)))) %>%
      # Reorder variables
      select(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), outcome, o_lab, o_cat, stat, contains("estimate"), base, base_description) 
  
    if(!is.null(conf)){
      if(conf == "ci"){
        table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% 
          mutate(estimate_ci = paste0(as.character(estimate_low), " - ", as.character(estimate_upp)))}
    } else { 
      table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% mutate(estimate_se = "-", .after = estimate) %>% 
        select(-estimate_low, -estimate_upp)
    }
    
    return(table_for_one_outcome)
    
  }) # Outcomes loop ends 
  
  # Stack tables one on another
  output <- output %>% bind_rows() %>% mutate(unweighted_n = base)
  
  # Either drop standard errors or confidence intervals
  if(!is.null(conf)){
  if(conf == "se"){keep <- c("upp", "low")} else {keep <- "_se"}
    output <- output %>% select(!ends_with(keep))
  }
  
  return(output)
  
})})
}

write_function(unweighted_mean, folder = "Scripts")



5.2.1.1 Trials
Just use data that is either class of numeric or factor.

5.2.1.1.1 Trial 1

trial <- unweighted_mean(df2, outcomes = c("libauth","leftrigh"), predictors = list("RSex"))
trial %>% print()

5.2.2 unweighted_median

unweighted_median <- function(
    data, # a data frame
    outcomes, # a character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, each element containing a character vector of predictor variables
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"; returns either standard error or confidence intervals 
    base = NULL
) {suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({
  
  output <- map(outcomes, function(cur_outcome) {
    
    base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = cur_outcome)) 
        
    # Remove missing data
    one_total_table <- data %>% 
      filter(!is.na(.data[[cur_outcome]])) %>% 
      summarise(estimate = median(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T),
                base = n())
      
    if(!is.null(conf)){
      if(conf == "se"){
      median_se <- wrappedtools::medianse(data %>% pull(cur_outcome))
      one_total_table <- one_total_table %>% mutate(estimate_se = median_se)
      } else {
      median_ci <- wrappedtools::median_cl_boot(data %>% pull(cur_outcome))
      one_total_table <- one_total_table %>% mutate(
        estimate_low = median_ci$CIlow,
        estimate_upp = median_ci$CIhigh)
      }
    } else {
      one_total_table <- one_total_table %>% mutate(estimate_se = "-")
    }
    
     one_total_table <- one_total_table %>% 
        mutate("{cur_outcome}" := "median") %>% 
        mutate(outcome = cur_outcome) %>% 
        rename(any_of(c(o_cat = cur_outcome))) %>% 
        mutate(o_lab = attributes(data[[cur_outcome]])$label) %>% 
        mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
  
    # Create cross-breaks
    if(!is.null(predictors)){
    predictor_tables <- map(predictors, function(cur_predictor) { 
      
            # Handle cases with and without predictor variables
        if (length(cur_predictor) > 0) {
          
          # Vector containing predictors and outcomes
          predictor_and_outcome <- c(cur_predictor, cur_outcome)
          base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = predictor_and_outcome)) # edit

          one_predictor_table <- data %>%
            # Filter missing values
            filter(rowSums(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome), ~ is.na(.))) == 0) %>%
            # Group data
            group_by(across(all_of(cur_predictor))) %>% 
            # Weighted mean + base sizes, with grouping vars
            summarise(estimate = median(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T),
                base = n())
          
      if(!is.null(conf)){
         if(conf == "se"){
           median_se <- grouped_medianse(data = data, predictors = cur_predictor, outcome = cur_outcome)
           one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% full_join(median_se)
          }
          else {
            median_ci <- grouped_medianci(data = data, predictors = cur_predictor, outcome = cur_outcome)
            one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% full_join(median_ci)
          }
      } else {
      one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% mutate(estimate_se = "-")
      }
      
          # Added for consistency when merged with categorical outcome tables. 
          one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% mutate("{cur_outcome}" := "median") %>% 
            # Standardise names so can stack results across different predictors and outcomes. 
            standardise_names(data = data, preds = cur_predictor, out_var = cur_outcome) %>%
            mutate(cross_break = paste0(cur_predictor, collapse = "_X_"))  %>%
            mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
            
        }
      
      
      return(one_predictor_table)
      
    })
    
    # Stack tables one on another
    predictor_tables <- bind_rows(predictor_tables)
    
    } # Predictors ends
    
    # Bind crossbreaks and frequency table together
    if(!is.null(predictors)){table_for_one_outcome <- bind_rows(one_total_table, predictor_tables)
    } else {table_for_one_outcome <- one_total_table}
    
    table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>%
      mutate(stat = "median") %>%
      # Add 'Total' to predictor category variables for frequency section of the table.
      mutate(across(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), ~case_when(is.na(.x) ~ "Total", TRUE ~ as.character(.x)))) %>%
      # Reorder variables
      select(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), outcome, o_lab, o_cat, stat, contains("estimate"), base, base_description) 
    
    if(!is.null(conf)){
    if(conf == "ci"){
      table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% 
        mutate(estimate_ci = paste0(as.character(estimate_low), " - ", as.character(estimate_upp)))
    }
    }
    
    return(table_for_one_outcome)
    
  }) # Outcomes loop ends 
  
  # Stack tables one on another
  output <- output %>% bind_rows() %>% mutate(unweighted_n = base)
  
  # Either drop standard errors or confidence intervals
  if(!is.null(conf)){
  if(conf == "se"){keep <- c("upp", "low")} else {keep <- "_se"}
    output <- output %>% select(!ends_with(keep))
  }
  
  return(output)
  
})})
}

write_function(unweighted_median, folder = "Scripts")

5.2.3 unweighted_sum

unweighted_sum <- function(
    data, # a data frame
    outcomes, # a character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, each element containing a character vector of predictor variables
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"; returns either standard error or confidence intervals 
    base = NULL
) {suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({
  
  output <- map(outcomes, function(cur_outcome) {
    
    base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = cur_outcome)) 

    # Remove missing data
    one_total_table <- data %>% 
      filter(!is.na(.data[[cur_outcome]])) %>% 
      summarise(estimate = sum(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
            base = n(), 
            sd = sd(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
            estimate_se = sd * sqrt(base), 
            estimate_low = estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se,
            estimate_upp = estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se
            ) %>% select(-sd)
    
     one_total_table <- one_total_table %>% 
        mutate("{cur_outcome}" := "sum") %>% 
        mutate(outcome = cur_outcome) %>% 
        rename(any_of(c(o_cat = cur_outcome))) %>% 
        mutate(o_lab = attributes(data[[cur_outcome]])$label) %>% 
        mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
  
    # Create cross-breaks
    if(!is.null(predictors)){
    predictor_tables <- map(predictors, function(cur_predictor) { 
      
            # Handle cases with and without predictor variables
        if (length(cur_predictor) > 0) {
          
          # Vector containing predictors and outcomes
          predictor_and_outcome <- c(cur_predictor, cur_outcome)
          base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = predictor_and_outcome)) 
          
          one_predictor_table <- data %>%
            # Filter missing values
            filter(rowSums(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome), ~ is.na(.))) == 0) %>%
            # Group data
            group_by(across(all_of(cur_predictor))) %>% 
            # Weighted mean + base sizes, with grouping vars
            summarise(estimate = sum(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
            base = n(), 
            sd = sd(!!sym(cur_outcome), na.rm = T), 
            estimate_se = sd * sqrt(base), 
            estimate_low = estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se,
            estimate_upp = estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se
            ) %>% select(-sd)
      
          # Added for consistency when merged with categorical outcome tables. 
          one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% mutate("{cur_outcome}" := "sum") %>% 
            # Standardise names so can stack results across different predictors and outcomes. 
            standardise_names(data = data, preds = cur_predictor, out_var = cur_outcome) %>%
            mutate(cross_break = paste0(cur_predictor, collapse = "_X_")) %>%
            mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
            
        }
      
      return(one_predictor_table)
      
    })
    
    # Stack tables one on another
    predictor_tables <- bind_rows(predictor_tables)
    
    } # Predictors ends
    
    # Bind crossbreaks and frequency table together
    if(!is.null(predictors)){table_for_one_outcome <- bind_rows(one_total_table, predictor_tables)
    } else {table_for_one_outcome <- one_total_table}
    
    table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>%
      mutate(stat = "sum") %>% 
      # Add 'Total' to predictor category variables for frequency section of the table.
      mutate(across(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), ~case_when(is.na(.x) ~ "Total", TRUE ~ as.character(.x)))) %>%
      # Reorder variables
      select(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), outcome, o_lab, o_cat, stat, contains("estimate"), base, base_description) 
    
    if(!is.null(conf)){
      if(conf == "ci"){
        table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% 
          mutate(estimate_ci = paste0(as.character(estimate_low), " - ", as.character(estimate_upp)))
      }
    } else {
      table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% mutate(estimate_se = "-") %>% 
        select(-estimate_upp, -estimate_low)
    }
    
    return(table_for_one_outcome)
    
  }) # Outcomes loop ends 
  
  # Stack tables one on another
  output <- output %>% bind_rows() %>% mutate(unweighted_n = base)
  
  # Either drop standard errors or confidence intervals
  if(!is.null(conf)){
  if(conf == "se"){keep <- c("upp", "low")} else {keep <- "_se"}
    output <- output %>% select(!ends_with(keep))
  }
  
  return(output)
  
})})
}

write_function(unweighted_sum, folder = "Scripts")

5.2.4 unweighted_perc

unweighted_perc <- function(
    data, # a data frame
    outcomes, # a character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, each element containing a character vector of predictor variables
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"; returns either standard error or confidence intervals 
    base = NULL
) {suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({
  
  output <- map(outcomes, function(cur_outcome) {
    
    base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = cur_outcome))

    # Remove missing data
    one_total_table <- data %>% 
      filter(!is.na(.data[[cur_outcome]])) %>% 
      count(.data[[cur_outcome]], name = "unweighted_n") %>%    
      mutate(estimate = unweighted_n / sum(unweighted_n), 
             estimate_se = sqrt(estimate * (1 - estimate) / sum(unweighted_n)), 
             estimate_low = (estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se),
             estimate_upp = (estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se),
             base = sum(unweighted_n, na.rm = T)
             ) %>% 
        mutate(outcome = cur_outcome) %>% 
        rename(any_of(c(o_cat = cur_outcome))) %>% 
        mutate(o_lab = attributes(data[[cur_outcome]])$label) %>% 
        mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
  
    # Create cross-breaks
    if(!is.null(predictors)){
    predictor_tables <- map(predictors, function(cur_predictor) { 
      
            # Handle cases with and without predictor variables
        if (length(cur_predictor) > 0) {
          
          # Vector containing predictors and outcomes
          predictor_and_outcome <- c(cur_predictor, cur_outcome)
          base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = predictor_and_outcome)) 
          
          one_predictor_table <- data %>%
            # Filter missing values
            filter(rowSums(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome), ~ is.na(.))) == 0) %>%
            # Group data
            group_by(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome))) %>% 
            summarise(unweighted_n = n()) %>%
            group_by(across(all_of(cur_predictor))) %>% 
            mutate(estimate = unweighted_n / sum(unweighted_n, na.rm = T), 
               estimate_se = sqrt(estimate * (1 - estimate) / sum(unweighted_n)), 
               estimate_low = (estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se),
               estimate_upp = (estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se),
               base = sum(unweighted_n, na.rm = T)
             )
      
          # Added for consistency when merged with categorical outcome tables. 
          one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% 
            # Standardise names so can stack results across different predictors and outcomes. 
            standardise_names(data = data, preds = cur_predictor, out_var = cur_outcome) %>%
            mutate(cross_break = paste0(cur_predictor, collapse = "_X_")) %>%
            mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
            
        }
      
      return(one_predictor_table)
      
    })
    
    # Stack tables one on another
    predictor_tables <- bind_rows(predictor_tables)
    
    } # Predictors ends
    
    # Bind crossbreaks and frequency table together
    if(!is.null(predictors)){table_for_one_outcome <- bind_rows(one_total_table, predictor_tables)
    } else {table_for_one_outcome <- one_total_table}
    
    table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>%
      mutate(stat = "perc") %>%
      # Add 'Total' to predictor category variables for frequency section of the table.
      mutate(across(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), ~case_when(is.na(.x) ~ "Total", TRUE ~ as.character(.x)))) %>%
      # Reorder variables
      select(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), outcome, o_lab, o_cat, stat, contains("estimate"), unweighted_n, base, base_description) 
    
    if(!is.null(conf)){
      if(conf == "ci"){
        table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% 
          mutate(estimate_ci = paste0(as.character(estimate_low), " - ", as.character(estimate_upp)))
      }
    } else {
      table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% mutate(estimate_se = "-") %>% 
        select(-estimate_upp, -estimate_low)
    }
    

    return(table_for_one_outcome)
    
  }) # Outcomes loop ends 
  
  # Stack tables one on another
  output <- output %>% bind_rows()
  
  # Either drop standard errors or confidence intervals
  if(!is.null(conf)){
  if(conf == "se"){keep <- c("upp", "low")} else {keep <- "_se"}
    output <- output %>% select(!ends_with(keep))
  }
  
  return(output)
  
})})
}

5.2.5 unweighted_count

unweighted_count <- function(
    data, # a data frame
    outcomes, # a character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, each element containing a character vector of predictor variables
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"; returns either standard error or confidence intervals 
    base = NULL
) {suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({
  
  output <- map(outcomes, function(cur_outcome) {
    
    base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = cur_outcome)) # edit

    # Remove missing data
    one_total_table <- data %>% 
      filter(!is.na(.data[[cur_outcome]])) %>% 
      count(.data[[cur_outcome]], name = "estimate") %>%    
      mutate(base = sum(estimate, na.rm = T), 
             prop = estimate / base,
             estimate_se = sqrt(base * prop * (1 - prop)),
             estimate_low = (estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se),
             estimate_upp = (estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se),
             ) %>% 
      select(-prop) %>% 
      mutate(outcome = cur_outcome) %>% 
      rename(any_of(c(o_cat = cur_outcome))) %>% 
      mutate(o_lab = attributes(data[[cur_outcome]])$label) %>% 
      mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
  
    # Create cross-breaks
    if(!is.null(predictors)){
    predictor_tables <- map(predictors, function(cur_predictor) { 
      
            # Handle cases with and without predictor variables
        if (length(cur_predictor) > 0) {
          
          # Vector containing predictors and outcomes
          predictor_and_outcome <- c(cur_predictor, cur_outcome)
          base_defintion <- ifelse(is.null(base), NA, create_bases(base_info = base, variables = predictor_and_outcome)) 
          
          one_predictor_table <- data %>%
            # Filter missing values
            filter(rowSums(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome), ~ is.na(.))) == 0) %>%
            # Group data
            group_by(across(all_of(predictor_and_outcome))) %>% 
            summarise(estimate = n()) %>%
            group_by(across(all_of(cur_predictor))) %>% 
            mutate(
              base = sum(estimate, na.rm = T),
              prop = estimate / sum(base), 
              estimate_se = sqrt(base * prop * (1 - prop)),
              estimate_low = (estimate - 1.96 * estimate_se),
              estimate_upp = (estimate + 1.96 * estimate_se)
             ) %>% select(-prop)
      
          # Added for consistency when merged with categorical outcome tables. 
          one_predictor_table <- one_predictor_table %>% 
            # Standardise names so can stack results across different predictors and outcomes. 
            standardise_names(data = data, preds = cur_predictor, out_var = cur_outcome) %>%
            mutate(cross_break = paste0(cur_predictor, collapse = "_X_")) %>%
            mutate(base_description = base_defintion) # descriptor label for base 
            
        }
      
      return(one_predictor_table)
      
    })
    
    # Stack tables one on another
    predictor_tables <- bind_rows(predictor_tables)
    
    } # Predictors ends
    
    # Bind crossbreaks and frequency table together
    if(!is.null(predictors)){table_for_one_outcome <- bind_rows(one_total_table, predictor_tables)
    } else {table_for_one_outcome <- one_total_table}
    
    table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>%
      mutate(stat = "count", unweighted_n = estimate) %>%
      # Add 'Total' to predictor category variables for frequency section of the table.
      mutate(across(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), ~case_when(is.na(.x) ~ "Total", TRUE ~ as.character(.x)))) %>%
      # Reorder variables
      select(contains(c("cross_break", "predictor", "p_lab", "p_cat")), outcome, o_lab, o_cat, stat, contains("estimate"), unweighted_n, base, base_description) 
    
    if(!is.null(conf)){
      if(conf == "ci"){
        table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% 
          mutate(estimate_ci = paste0(as.character(estimate_low), " - ", as.character(estimate_upp)))
      }
    } else {
      table_for_one_outcome <- table_for_one_outcome %>% mutate(estimate_se = "-") %>% 
        select(-estimate_upp, -estimate_low)
    }
    
    

    return(table_for_one_outcome)
    
  }) # Outcomes loop ends 
  
  # Stack tables one on another
  output <- output %>% bind_rows()
  
  # Either drop standard errors or confidence intervals
  if(!is.null(conf)){
  if(conf == "se"){keep <- c("upp", "low")} else {keep <- "_se"}
    output <- output %>% select(!ends_with(keep))
  }
  
  return(output)
  
})})
}

write_function(unweighted_count, folder = "Scripts")

title: 8  Multicodes
text:
8.2 Identify Multicodes

8.2.1 Steps overview
This code identifies variables with: a) a stem divider, I will use “:”, b) a matching set of values on the left hand side of the stem divider, c) No more than 2 unique values in its levels, and d) the same base size.
So, the variable labels will be split in two around “:”. A multicode set is one where all the left hand sides of the resulting variable are the same.
But how to identify matching sets?
Once done it will replace variable label with the left hand part of the stem.
The response levels will be filtered to ‘yes’ or equivalent. ‘yes’ will be a specifiable argument, e.g. could be ‘mentioned’.
For simplicity, I’ll assume binaries be coded consistently, e.g. people don’t feed in a combination of yes/no and mentioned/not mentioned questions.
It will replace variable level == ‘yes’ with the right hand part of the stem.
Variable name will be reduced to the stem that is consistent across all variables in that set. Not dealt yet with the possibility that there is no consistent stem.
Base description will be updated to have a single base description for the variable as a whole, using the left hand part of the stem as the variable label.
This new base description will be silently added to the object with base description in it, in these trials that is example_of_base_information.
Should I then drop duplicates from the base description info? Probably, yes.



8.2.2 Test data

8.2.3 Convenience function to reduce variable names to a common stem
This takes a vector of variable names and returns a single character value with the common parts of the strings in that vector, assuming there is one.

# Example vector of multi-coded variable names
vars <- c("Q1_OptionA", "Q1_OptionB", "Q1_OptionC", "Q1_OptionD", "Q1_OptionE")

# Find longest common prefix
common_prefix <- function(x) {
  split_names <- strsplit(x, "")
  min_length <- min(lengths(split_names))
  prefix <- character(0)
  for (i in seq_len(min_length)) {
    chars <- sapply(split_names, `[`, i)
    if (length(unique(chars)) == 1) {
      prefix <- c(prefix, chars[1])
    } else {
      break
    }
  }
  paste0(prefix, collapse = "")
}

write_function(common_prefix, folder = "Scripts")

common_prefix(vars)

common_prefix(set1)

8.2.4 Intial variable screening
The first stage narrows the data frame received from calc_stats down to suitable rows:

stat must equal “perc” or “count”,

o_lab must contain “:”,

The outcome variable must contain either 1 or 2 levels,

Those levels must include the relevant value, for now “yes”. Could be user specified.



For these rows of data it them creates a variable called left_stem, which contains the values on the left hand side of “:”. This will be the variable label for the new multicode variable.



There must be more than one variable with a consistent left_stem value.

The base value must be the same within unique values of left_stem.

The resulting output is a dataframe with only the relevant rows of data from the output of calc_stats.

multis <- trial2 %>% 
  filter(stat == "perc" | stat == "count") %>% # Multicodes relevant only for categorical data
  filter(grepl(": ", o_lab)) %>% # Label must contain ": "
  group_by(across(contains(c("cross_break", "p_cat"))), outcome) %>% # Group by outcome variable
  mutate(lev_num = max(row_number())) %>% # Count number of levels in each variable
  group_by(outcome) %>% 
  mutate(lev_relevant = case_when(any(o_cat == "Yes") ~ 1, TRUE ~ 0)) %>% # Any of variable's levels equal yes? 
  filter(
    lev_num <= 2 & # Only use variables with 1 or 2 levels
    lev_relevant == 1 # Only use variables with a relevant level, here 'yes'
    )  %>% 
  mutate(
    left_stem = str_split_i(o_lab, ": ", 1) # Create left variable label stem 
    ) %>% 
  group_by(across(contains(c("crossbreak", "p_cat"))), left_stem) %>% # Group by predictors and left stem
  mutate(
    stem_count = n_distinct(outcome), # number of different variables with that variable stem 
    base_count = n_distinct(base) # Number of unique values in base
    ) %>% 
  filter(stem_count > 1) %>% # Only use variables where right stem recurs more than once
  filter(base_count == 1)  # Only use variables where base is unique within left_stem

8.2.5 Reorganise data identified as multicoded

# The next step is done separately for each unique multicode set and predictor combination 
# Identified with left_stem2, created below. 
# Results returned as a list from map()

# If the column "cross_break" exists in the dataframe 'multis'...
if("cross_break" %in% names(multis)){
  # ...then create a new column 'left_stem2' by combining 'left_stem' and 'cross_break'
  multis <- multis %>% mutate(left_stem2 = paste0(left_stem, " - ", cross_break))
} else {
  # ...otherwise, just copy 'left_stem' into 'left_stem2'
  multis <- multis %>% mutate(left_stem2 = left_stem)
}

# ℹ 90 more rows
# ℹ 12 more variables: estimate_se <dbl>, unweighted_n <int>, base <int>,
#   base_description <chr>, p_method <lgl>, p_value <lgl>, lev_num <int>,
#   lev_relevant <dbl>, left_stem <chr>, stem_count <int>, base_count <int>,
#   left_stem2 <chr>

# The following line is commented out — for testing 
# stem <- unique_stems[2]


# Get all unique values of 'left_stem2' in the data
unique_stems <- multis$left_stem2 %>% unique

# Loop over each unique value of 'left_stem2'
result <- map(unique_stems, function(stem) {
  
  # Filter 'multis' to only the rows matching the current stem
  multis2 <- multis %>% 
    filter(left_stem2 == stem) 
  
  # Save the original variable label from the first row (for later use)
  old_variable_label <- multis2$o_lab[1]
  
  # Step 1: Create a 'right_stem' by splitting 'o_lab' on ": " and taking the second part
  # Step 2: Replace 'o_lab' with the value of 'left_stem'
  multis2 <- multis2 %>%
    mutate(
      right_stem = str_split_i(o_lab, ": ", 2), 
      o_lab = left_stem
    ) %>% 
    # Step 3: Keep only rows where 'o_cat' == "Yes"
    filter(o_cat == "Yes") %>% 
    # Step 4: Replace 'o_cat' with 'right_stem' values, and shorten 'outcome' to its common prefix
    mutate(
      o_cat = right_stem, 
      outcome = common_prefix(outcome)
    ) 
  
  # Get the unique new variable name
  new_variable_name <- multis2$outcome %>% unique()
  # Get the unique new variable label
  new_variable_label <- multis2$o_lab %>% unique
  # Name the label vector with the new variable name
  names(new_variable_label) <- new_variable_name
  
  # Get the existing base description from the first row
  existing_base_description <- multis2$base_description[1]
  
  # If the base description contains " X ", split it into two parts
  if(str_detect(existing_base_description, " X ")){
    existing_base_description_2 <- str_split(existing_base_description, " X ") %>% unlist
  } else {
    # Otherwise, keep it as is
    existing_base_description_2 <- existing_base_description
  }
  
  # Create a logical index for which part(s) match the old variable label
  index <- str_detect(existing_base_description_2, old_variable_label)
  reverse_index <- !str_detect(existing_base_description_2, old_variable_label)
  
  # Extract the matching part(s) and remove anything after ":- "
  existing_base_description_3 <- existing_base_description_2[index] %>% str_split_i(., ":- ", 1)
  
  # Create a revised base label by appending the new variable label
  revised_base_label <- paste0(existing_base_description_3, ":- ", new_variable_label)
  names(revised_base_label) <- new_variable_name
  
  # If the base description originally had " X ", reassemble with the unchanged part(s)
  if(str_detect(existing_base_description, " X ")){
    remainder_of_base_description <- existing_base_description_2[reverse_index] 
    revised_base_description <- paste(
      revised_base_label, 
      remainder_of_base_description, 
      sep = " X ")
  } else {
    revised_base_description <- revised_base_label
  }
  
  # Update 'base_description' in the filtered dataset
  multis2 <- multis2 %>% mutate(base_description = revised_base_description)
  
  # Logical test: stop execution if the new variable name already exists in 'example_of_base_information2'
  if (new_variable_name %in% names(example_of_base_information2)) {
    stop("Error: New multicode variable name already in data.")
  } 
  
  # Return a list containing:
  # 1. The updated 'multis2' dataframe
  # 2. The revised base label
  # 3. The new variable label
  return(list(multis2, revised_base_label, new_variable_label))
  
}) 
# End of map() loop

# Combine all first elements of 'result' (the dataframes) into one big dataframe
tables <- bind_rows(map(result, ~(.x[[1]])))

# Extract all base labels from the results, flatten into one vector, and remove duplicates
new_base_descriptors <- map(result, ~(.x[[2]])) %>% unlist
new_base_descriptors <- new_base_descriptors[!duplicated(new_base_descriptors)]

# Extract all variable labels from the results, flatten, and remove duplicates
new_variable_labels <- map(result, ~(.x[[3]])) %>% unlist
new_variable_labels <- new_variable_labels[!duplicated(new_variable_labels)]

# Make a temporary copy of 'example_of_base_information2'
example_of_base_information2_temp <- example_of_base_information2

# Append new base descriptors and labels to positions [[2]], [[3]], and [[4]] of that object
example_of_base_information2_temp[[2]] <- c(example_of_base_information2[[2]], new_base_descriptors)
example_of_base_information2_temp[[3]] <- c(example_of_base_information2[[3]], new_variable_labels)
example_of_base_information2_temp[[4]] <- c(example_of_base_information2[[4]], new_variable_labels)

# Overwrite the original 'example_of_base_information2' object in the global environment with the updated version
example_of_base_information2 <<- example_of_base_information2_temp

multis2 <- tables %>% ungroup() %>% select(-lev_num, -lev_relevant, -left_stem, -stem_count, -base_count, -left_stem2, -right_stem)

8.2.6 Fuction

  # filter(stat == "perc" | stat == "count") %>% # Multicodes relevant only for categorical data
  # filter(grepl(": ", o_lab)) %>% # Label must contain ": "
  # group_by(across(contains(c("cross_break", "p_cat"))), outcome) %>% # Group by outcome variable
  # mutate(lev_num = max(row_number())) %>% # Count number of levels in each variable
  # group_by(outcome) %>% 
  # mutate(lev_relevant = case_when(any(o_cat == "Yes") ~ 1, TRUE ~ 0)) %>% # Any of variable's levels equal yes? 

# data <- trial3
# base_info <- example_of_base_information2
# rm(data)
# rm(base_info)
# rm(keep)

convert_multicodes <- function(
  data, 
  base_info = NULL, 
  keep = "Yes"){
  
  if(!is.null(base_info)){base_info_name_as_string <- deparse(substitute(base_info))}
  
  multis <- data %>% 
    filter(stat == "perc" | stat == "count" | stat == "w_count" | stat == "w_perc") %>% # Multicodes relevant only for categorical data
    filter(grepl(": ", o_lab)) %>% # Label must contain ": "
    
    # Count number of levels in each variable
    group_by(across(contains(c("cross_break", "p_cat"))), outcome) %>% # Group by outcome variable + crossbreak set + levels
    mutate(lev_num = max(row_number())) %>% 
    
    # Any of variable's levels equal yes?
    group_by(outcome) %>% 
    mutate(lev_relevant = case_when(any(o_cat == "Yes") ~ 1, TRUE ~ 0)) %>%  
    
    # filter non-relevant variables
    filter(
      lev_num <= 2 & # Only use variables with 1 or 2 levels
      lev_relevant == 1 # Only use variables with a relevant level, here 'yes'
      )  %>% 
    mutate(
      left_stem = str_split_i(o_lab, ": ", 1) # Create left variable label stem 
      ) %>% 
    group_by(across(contains(c("crossbreak", "p_cat"))), left_stem) %>% # Group by predictors and left stem
    mutate(
      stem_count = n_distinct(outcome), # number of different variables with that variable stem 
      base_count = n_distinct(base) # Number of unique values in base
      ) %>% 
    filter(stem_count > 1) %>% # Only use variables where right stem recurs more than once
    filter(base_count == 1)  # Only use variables where base is unique within left_stem
  
  if(nrow(multis) > 0){
  
  # Create an identifier vector for multicode rows from: 
  #  -  cross_break (all the predictors), 
  #  -  and the outcome variable.
  # Then reduce that to a vector of unique values
  multicode_row_identifiers <-  multis %>% unite(ident, contains(c("cross_break", "outcome"))) %>% pull(ident) %>% unique
  multicode_row_identifiers
  
  # Use `multicode_row_identifiers` to drop these rows from the original table.
  single_codes <- data %>% unite(ident, contains(c("cross_break", "outcome")), remove = F) %>% 
    filter(!ident %in% multicode_row_identifiers) %>% 
    select(-ident)

  # The next step is done separately for each unique multicode set and predictor combination 
  # Identified with left_stem2, created below. 
  # Results returned as a list from map()
  
  # If the column "cross_break" exists in the dataframe 'multis'...
  if("cross_break" %in% names(multis)){
    # ...then create a new column 'left_stem2' by combining 'left_stem' and 'cross_break'
    multis <- multis %>% mutate(left_stem2 = paste0(left_stem, " - ", cross_break))
  } else {
    # ...otherwise, just copy 'left_stem' into 'left_stem2'
    multis <- multis %>% mutate(left_stem2 = left_stem)
  }
  
  # Get all unique values of 'left_stem2' in the data
  unique_stems <- multis$left_stem2 %>% unique
  # stem <- unique_stems[[2]]
  
    # Loop over each unique value of 'left_stem2'
    result <- map(unique_stems, function(stem) {
    
    # Filter 'multis' to only the rows matching the current stem
    multis2 <- multis %>% filter(left_stem2 == stem) 
    
    # Save the original variable label from the first row (for later use)
    old_variable_label <- multis2$o_lab[1]
    
    # Step 1: Create a 'right_stem' by splitting 'o_lab' on ": " and taking the second part
    # Step 2: Replace 'o_lab' with the value of 'left_stem'
    multis2 <- multis2 %>% ungroup() %>%
      mutate(
        right_stem = str_split_i(o_lab, ": ", 2), 
        o_lab = left_stem
      ) %>% 
      # Step 3: Keep only rows where 'o_cat' == "Yes"
      filter(o_cat == "Yes") %>% 
      # Step 4: Replace 'o_cat' with 'right_stem' values, and shorten 'outcome' to its common prefix
      mutate(
        o_cat = right_stem, 
        outcome = common_prefix(outcome)
      ) 
    
    # Skip base description related steps where base_info NULL
    if(!is.null(base_info)){
        
      # Get the unique new variable name
      new_variable_name <- multis2$outcome %>% unique()
      # Get the unique new variable label
      new_variable_label <- multis2$o_lab %>% unique
      # Name the label vector with the new variable name
      names(new_variable_label) <- new_variable_name
      
      # Get the existing base description from the first row
      existing_base_description <- multis2$base_description[1]
      
      # If the base description contains " X ", split it into two parts
      if(str_detect(existing_base_description, " X ")){
        existing_base_description_2 <- str_split(existing_base_description, " X ") %>% unlist
      } else {
        # Otherwise, keep it as is
        existing_base_description_2 <- existing_base_description
      }
      
      # Create a logical index for which part(s) match the old variable label
      index <- str_detect(existing_base_description_2, old_variable_label)
      reverse_index <- !str_detect(existing_base_description_2, old_variable_label)
      
      # Extract the matching part(s) and remove anything after ":- "
      existing_base_description_3 <- existing_base_description_2[index] %>% str_split_i(., ":- ", 1)
      
      # Create a revised base label by appending the new variable label
      revised_base_label <- paste0(existing_base_description_3, ":- ", new_variable_label)
      names(revised_base_label) <- new_variable_name
      
      # If the base description originally had " X ", reassemble with the unchanged part(s)
      if(str_detect(existing_base_description, " X ")){
        remainder_of_base_description <- existing_base_description_2[reverse_index] 
        revised_base_description <- paste0(
          c(revised_base_label, remainder_of_base_description), 
          collapse = " X ")
      } else {
        revised_base_description <- revised_base_label
      }
      
      # Update 'base_description' in the filtered dataset
      multis2 <- multis2 %>% mutate(base_description = revised_base_description)
      
      # Logical test: drop existing variable names from 'base_info'
      if (new_variable_name %in% names(base_info[[3]])) {
        
        keep_item_2 <- which(!(base_info[[2]] %>% names()) %in% new_variable_name)
        keep_item_3 <- which(!(base_info[[3]] %>% names()) %in% new_variable_name)
        keep_item_4 <- which(!(base_info[[4]] %>% names()) %in% new_variable_name)
        base_info[[2]] <- base_info[[2]][keep_item_2]
        base_info[[3]] <- base_info[[3]][keep_item_3]
        base_info[[4]] <- base_info[[4]][keep_item_4]

        # stop("Error: New multicode variable name already in data.")
      } 
      
      # Where base description provided, return a list containing:
      #  1. The updated 'multis2' dataframe
      #  2. The revised base label
      #  3. The new variable label
      return(list(multis2, revised_base_label, new_variable_label))
    
    } else {
      
      return(list(multis2)) # Otherwise, simply return data frame, in a list so map() below will work
      
    }
    
    }) # End of map() loop
  
  # Combine all first elements of 'result' (the dataframes) into one big dataframe
  tables <- bind_rows(map(result, ~(.x[[1]])))
  
  # Skip base description related steps where base_info NULL
  if(!is.null(base_info)){
    
    # Extract all base labels from the results, flatten into one vector, and remove duplicates
    new_base_descriptors <- map(result, ~(.x[[2]])) %>% unlist
    new_base_descriptors <- new_base_descriptors[!duplicated(new_base_descriptors)]
    
    # Extract all variable labels from the results, flatten, and remove duplicates
    new_variable_labels <- map(result, ~(.x[[3]])) %>% unlist
    new_variable_labels <- new_variable_labels[!duplicated(new_variable_labels)]
    
    # Make a temporary copy of 'base_info'
    example_of_base_info2_temp <- base_info
    
    # If those variable names already exist in base descriptions information, then drop them...
    base_info[[2]] <- base_info[[2]][which(!base_info[[2]] %in% new_base_descriptors)]
    base_info[[3]] <- base_info[[3]][which(!base_info[[3]] %in% new_variable_labels)]
    base_info[[4]] <- base_info[[4]][which(!base_info[[4]] %in% new_variable_labels)]

    # Append new base descriptors and labels to positions [[2]], [[3]], and [[4]] of that object
    example_of_base_info2_temp[[2]] <- c(base_info[[2]], new_base_descriptors)
    example_of_base_info2_temp[[3]] <- c(base_info[[3]], new_variable_labels)
    example_of_base_info2_temp[[4]] <- c(base_info[[4]], new_variable_labels)
    
    # Overwrite the original 'base_info' object in the global environment with the updated version
    assign(base_info_name_as_string, example_of_base_info2_temp, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    # base_info <<- example_of_base_info2_temp
  
  }
  
  # Create multicode object to export
  multis2 <- tables %>% 
    ungroup() %>% # Otherwise, cannot drop left_stem
    # Drop temporary variables
    select(-lev_num, -lev_relevant, -left_stem, -stem_count, -base_count, -left_stem2, -right_stem)
  
  # Bind single and multicode data together
  multicoded_and_single_code_data <- bind_rows(single_codes, multis2)
  
  return(multicoded_and_single_code_data)
  
  } else {return(data)}
  
}

write_function(convert_multicodes, folder = "Scripts")

title: 7  Calculate statistics
text:
7.2 Error testing

Handle nested predictorsConfirm list depthConfirm variables existConfirm predictors are factors.Confirm outcomes not in predictors list


Examples of outcome structures. nested_predictors won’t work, will cause an error. predictors_one_level is one that will work, a single level list, each element a character vector of variable length.

nested_predictors <- 
list(
  list("MuchPov", "RSex", "RAgeCat"), 
  list(c("skipmeal", "RAgeCat"), "MuchPov2")
) 

nested_predictors

[[1]]
[[1]][[1]]
[1] "MuchPov"

[[1]][[2]]
[1] "RSex"

[[1]][[3]]
[1] "RAgeCat"


[[2]]
[[2]][[1]]
[1] "skipmeal" "RAgeCat" 

[[2]][[2]]
[1] "MuchPov2"

predictors_one_level <- list(c("skipmeal", "RAgeCat"), "MuchPov2")

predictors_one_level

[[1]]
[1] "skipmeal" "RAgeCat" 

[[2]]
[1] "MuchPov2"





This function then returns list depth. If depth equals 1 then use same predictors for all outcomes. If depth equals 2 then try and use different sets of predictors for different outcomes.

list_depth <- function(x) {
  if (!is.list(x)) {
    return(0)  # Not a list → depth 0
  } else if (length(x) == 0) {
    return(1)  # Empty list → depth 1
  } else {
    return(1 + max(sapply(x, list_depth)))
  }
}

write_function(list_depth, folder = "Scripts")

# Use for test to ensure list depth = 1. 
list_depth(nested_predictors)

[1] 2

list_depth(predictors_one_level)

[1] 1






# Returns TRUE if all variables in list appear in data
# Otherwise returns FALSE
vars_exist <- function(variable_list, data){
  
  if(any(grepl("survey", class(data)))){
    df_to_check <- data[["variables"]]
    } else {
      df_to_check <- data
      }
  
  predictors <- variable_list %>% unlist %>% unique
  variables_in_df <- names(df_to_check)
  
  variables_in_both <- intersect(predictors, variables_in_df)
  
  length(predictors) == length(variables_in_both)

  }

vars_exist(data = df2, variable_list = nested_predictors)

[1] TRUE

vars_exist(data = df_svy, variable_list = nested_predictors)

[1] TRUE

vars_exist(data = df2, variable_list = list("Random text")) # Random text not a variable name. 

[1] FALSE

write_function(vars_exist, folder = "Scripts")





check_all_factors <- function(data, variable_list){

  if(any(grepl("survey", class(data)))){
    df_to_check <- data[["variables"]]
    } else {
      df_to_check <- data
      }
  
  df_to_check %>% select(all_of(unlist(variable_list))) %>%
    map_chr(., ~paste0(class(.x), collapse = ", ")) %>% 
    unique == "factor"
}

check_all_factors(data = df2, variable_list = nested_predictors)

[1] TRUE

check_all_factors(data = df_svy, variable_list = nested_predictors)

[1] TRUE

write_function(check_all_factors, folder = "Scripts")




Returns TRUE when no overlap between predictors and outcomes

(vector_of_outcomes = c("libauth","welfare2", "leftrigh"))

[1] "libauth"  "welfare2" "leftrigh"

predictors_one_level

[[1]]
[1] "skipmeal" "RAgeCat" 

[[2]]
[1] "MuchPov2"

outcomes_not_in_predictors <- function(outcomes, predictors){

  predictor_vector <- unlist(predictors) %>% unique
  overlap_of_predictors_and_outcomes <- intersect(outcomes, predictor_vector)
  length(overlap_of_predictors_and_outcomes) == 0
  
}

outcomes_not_in_predictors(vector_of_outcomes, predictors_one_level)

[1] TRUE

write_function(outcomes_not_in_predictors, folder = "Scripts")

title: 7  Calculate statistics
text:
7.4 Wrapper to combine things
Checks:

Confirm outcomes not in list of predictors outcomes_not_in_predictors.


calc_stats <- function(
    data,
    outcomes, # Character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, usually each element contains a character vector of predictor variables
    statistics = c("count","mean"),  # default, unweighted count or mean
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"
    base = NULL, 
    pval = NULL, # Whether to compute p-values (if not NULL)
    multicode = TRUE
    ){
  
  # Correct common, non-harmful, and inevitable mistakes #######################
  
  # If `predictors` is a character vector put it in a list
  if(class(predictors) == "character"){predictors <- list(predictors)}
  
  
  # Error handling #############################################################

  # Check variables do, in fact, exist. 
  vector_of_variables_concerned <- c(outcomes, unlist(unique(predictors)))
  test <- vars_exist(data = data, variable_list = vector_of_variables_concerned)
  if (test != TRUE) stop("Some variables you want to use do not exist in the data frame.")

  if(!is.null(predictors)){
    
  # Check outcomes not in predictors
  test <- outcomes_not_in_predictors(outcomes = outcomes, predictors = predictors)
  if (test != TRUE) stop("Outcome appears in predictor list.")

  # Confirm predictors are factors
    test <- check_all_factors(data = data, variable_list = predictors)
    if (test != TRUE) stop("Not all predictors are factor variables. Make them factors.")
  
  # Check predictor list depth is 1
  test <- list_depth(predictors) == 1
  if (test != TRUE) stop("Predictor list should not contained further lists.")
  }
  
  suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({ 
    
  # Where necessary, extract unweighted data from survey objects  
  if(any(str_detect(class(data), "survey"))){
    unweighted_data <- data[["variables"]]
    weighted_data <- data
  } else {
      unweighted_data <- data
      weighted_data <- NULL
  }
    
  # Compute statistics for each outcome/predictor combination 
  individual_tables <- pmap(list(outcomes), function(cur_outcome){
    
    # Extract current outcome variable and filter statistics based on variable type
    single_outcome <- unweighted_data %>% pull(cur_outcome) 
    if(is.factor(single_outcome)){statistic <- str_remove_all(statistics, "mean|median|sum")}
    if(is.numeric(single_outcome)){statistic <- str_remove_all(statistics, "perc|count")}
    
    results <- list() # Store stat results for this combo

    if("mean" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_mean(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("median" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_median(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("sum" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_sum(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("perc" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_perc(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("count" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_count(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    
    if(!is.null(weighted_data)){
    if("w_mean" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_mean(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_median" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_median(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_sum" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_sum(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_perc" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_perc(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_count" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_count(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    }
    
    # Combine results for this outcome
    if(length(results) > 0){results <- results %>% reduce(bind_rows)}

    return(results)
  
  })

  # Merge all results into one table 
  merged_tables <- individual_tables %>% bind_rows
  
  if(!is.null(pval)){
    
    # Extract predictors eligible for p-value testing (only single variables)
    pvalue_preds <- predictors[predictors %>% map_lgl(., ~length(.x) == 1)]
    pvalue_preds <- pvalue_preds %>% unlist %>% unique
    
  pvalues <- list()
  
  # Unweighted p-values
  if(any(statistics %in% c("mean","median","sum","perc","count"))){
    unw_pvalues <- map_return_p_values(data = unweighted_data, outcomes  = outcomes, predictors = pvalue_preds)
    pvalues <- append(pvalues, list(unw_pvalues))
  }

  # Weighted p-values
  if(!is.null(weighted_data)){
    if(any(statistics %in% c("w_mean", "w_median", "w_sum", "w_perc", "w_count"))){
      wgt_pvalues <- map_return_p_values(data = data, outcomes  = outcomes, predictors = pvalue_preds)
      pvalues <- append(pvalues, list(wgt_pvalues))
      }
  }
  
  # Merge p-values into main results
  pvalues <- pvalues %>% bind_rows()
  merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% left_join(pvalues)
  
  } else { 
    # If no p-values requested, still add empty columns
    merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% mutate(p_method = NA, p_value = NA)
  }
  
  # Handle the case of no predictors (total summary) 
  if(is.null(predictors)){merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% mutate(cross_break = "Total")}
  
  # Convert multi-coded values using base info
  # Inserted if statement so that if mulitcodes functions not available, can be excluded. 
  if(multicode == T){
  merged_tables <- convert_multicodes(
    data = merged_tables,  
    base_info = base, 
    keep = "Yes"
    ) 
  }

  return(merged_tables)
  
})})
}

write_function(calc_stats, folder = "Scripts")



7.4.0.1 Tests

Test 1Test 2Test 3Test 4.Test 5Test 6Test 7Test 8Test 9



test1 <- calc_stats(data = df_svy, 
           outcomes = c("RAgeCat","libauth", "welfare3"), 
           predictors = list(c("RSex","skipmeal2","MuchPov2"), "RSex"),
           statistics = c("w_perc","mean"), 
           base = example_of_base_information,
           conf = "ci", pval = T) 


test1 %>% print(n=1000)

test1 %>% pull(p_value)





calc_stats(data = df2, 
           outcomes = list("libauth","welfare2", "leftrigh"),

title: 7  Calculate statistics
text:
7.3 Wrapper to combine things - calc_stats
Checks:

Confirm outcomes not in list of predictors outcomes_not_in_predictors.


calc_stats <- function(
    data,
    outcomes, # Character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, usually each element contains a character vector of predictor variables
    statistics = c("count","mean"),  # default, unweighted count or mean
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"
    base = NULL, 
    pval = NULL, # Whether to compute p-values (if not NULL)
    multicode = TRUE
    ){
  
  # Correct common, non-harmful, and inevitable mistakes #######################
  
  # If `predictors` is a character vector put it in a list
  if(class(predictors) == "character"){predictors <- list(predictors)}
  
  
  # Error handling #############################################################

  # Check variables do, in fact, exist. 
  vector_of_variables_concerned <- c(outcomes, unlist(unique(predictors)))
  test <- vars_exist(data = data, variable_list = vector_of_variables_concerned)
  if (test != TRUE) stop("Some variables you want to use do not exist in the data frame.")

  if(!is.null(predictors)){
    
  # Check outcomes not in predictors
  test <- outcomes_not_in_predictors(outcomes = outcomes, predictors = predictors)
  if (test != TRUE) stop("Outcome appears in predictor list.")

  # Confirm predictors are factors
    test <- check_all_factors(data = data, variable_list = predictors)
    if (test != TRUE) stop("Not all predictors are factor variables. Make them factors.")
  
  # Check predictor list depth is 1
  test <- list_depth(predictors) == 1
  if (test != TRUE) stop("Predictor list should not contained further lists.")
  }
  
  suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({ 
    
  # Where necessary, extract unweighted data from survey objects  
  if(any(str_detect(class(data), "survey"))){
    unweighted_data <- data[["variables"]]
    weighted_data <- data
  } else {
      unweighted_data <- data
      weighted_data <- NULL
  }
    
  # Compute statistics for each outcome/predictor combination 
  individual_tables <- pmap(list(outcomes), function(cur_outcome){
    
    # Extract current outcome variable and filter statistics based on variable type
    single_outcome <- unweighted_data %>% pull(cur_outcome) 
    if(is.factor(single_outcome)){statistic <- str_remove_all(statistics, "mean|median|sum")}
    if(is.numeric(single_outcome)){statistic <- str_remove_all(statistics, "perc|count")}
    
    results <- list() # Store stat results for this combo

    if("mean" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_mean(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("median" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_median(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("sum" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_sum(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("perc" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_perc(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("count" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_count(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    
    if(!is.null(weighted_data)){
    if("w_mean" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_mean(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_median" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_median(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_sum" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_sum(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_perc" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_perc(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_count" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_count(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictors, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    }
    
    # Combine results for this outcome
    if(length(results) > 0){results <- results %>% reduce(bind_rows)}

    return(results)
  
  })

  # Merge all results into one table 
  merged_tables <- individual_tables %>% bind_rows
  
  if(!is.null(pval)){
    
    # Extract predictors eligible for p-value testing (only single variables)
    pvalue_preds <- predictors[predictors %>% map_lgl(., ~length(.x) == 1)]
    pvalue_preds <- pvalue_preds %>% unlist %>% unique
    
  pvalues <- list()
  
  # Unweighted p-values
  if(any(statistics %in% c("mean","median","sum","perc","count"))){
    unw_pvalues <- map_return_p_values(data = unweighted_data, outcomes  = outcomes, predictors = pvalue_preds)
    pvalues <- append(pvalues, list(unw_pvalues))
  }

  # Weighted p-values
  if(!is.null(weighted_data)){
    if(any(statistics %in% c("w_mean", "w_median", "w_sum", "w_perc", "w_count"))){
      wgt_pvalues <- map_return_p_values(data = data, outcomes  = outcomes, predictors = pvalue_preds)
      pvalues <- append(pvalues, list(wgt_pvalues))
      }
  }
  
  # Merge p-values into main results
  pvalues <- pvalues %>% bind_rows()
  merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% left_join(pvalues)
  
  } else { 
    # If no p-values requested, still add empty columns
    merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% mutate(p_method = NA, p_value = NA)
  }
  
  # Handle the case of no predictors (total summary) 
  if(is.null(predictors)){merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% mutate(cross_break = "Total")}
  
  # Convert multi-coded values using base info
  # Inserted if statement so that if mulitcodes functions not available, can be excluded. 
  if(multicode == T){
  merged_tables <- convert_multicodes(
    data = merged_tables,  
    base_info = base, 
    keep = "Yes"
    ) 
  }

  return(merged_tables)
  
})})
}

write_function(calc_stats, folder = "Scripts")

title: 3  Overview
text:
3.2 Main supporting functions

3.2.1 Base descriptions
Consists of three functions. base_information collects a set of base descriptions linked to variable names. create_bases selects a set of those bases for a particular combination of variables used in a table, and saves them in the output in the base_description column. prepare_base_for_table then puts it in a semi-readable format for a table.


3.2.2 Multicodes
This checks to see if there is a common stem among some of the variables, which must be the case in both variable and variable labels. If there is, it reduces the variable names to that common stem and splits the variable label in two, taking the common stem to become the new variable label and placing the unique parts in the position of categories, over-writing ‘yes’. The ‘no’ responses are then filtered out. This is only done where all variables with the common stem also have the same base size. The result is an output that in the data frame appears to relate to a single variable and can afterwards be treated as one. It also includes a step to combine their base labels into a single label - really these should all be the same anyway.

title: 3  Overview
text:
3.3 A wrapper - calc_stats
Arguments, mostly the same as in the individual functions.
pval - if not NULL, then compute p-values for single predictor. No method for nested variables.
multicode - this calls a supporting function to identify and format multicode variables.
statistics - specifices which statistics to calculate. The allowed values allowed are:

“mean”

“median”

“sum”

“perc”

“count”

“w_mean”

“w_median”

“w_sum”

“w_perc”

“w_count”

Accepts multiple arguments. By default… c(“count”,“mean”).
Data output structure:- remains the same as for individual statistic functions with two additions:

p_method - the description of what significance test has been run,

p_value - a p-value, when you have a single predictor, this will be the output.

make_hux <- function(table){
  
  (predictor_label <- table %>% select(p_lab1) %>% filter(p_lab1 != "Total") %>% unlist %>% unique)
  
  (n_predictor_levels <- table %>% select(p_cat1) %>% filter(p_cat1 != "Total") %>% unlist %>% unique %>% length)
  
  (preds_df <- tibble(var = rep(predictor_label, n_predictor_levels)) %>% t() %>% as.data.frame() %>% unname)

  ht <- as_hux(preds_df, add_colnames = FALSE)
  ht <- merge_cells(ht, 1, 1:n_predictor_levels)
  ht <- insert_column(ht, "", after = 0)
  ht <- insert_column(ht, "", after = 0)
  
  return(ht)
  
}

top_of_table <- make_hux(table = tbl) 

top_of_table %>% huxtable::theme_striped()

  mutate_if(is.numeric, ~round(.x * 100, 1)) %>% 
  mutate(ci = paste0("[", estimate_low, "-", estimate_upp, "]")) %>% 
  select(-estimate_low, -estimate_upp) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = p_cat1, values_from = c(estimate, ci), names_sep = "__") %>% 
  mutate_if(is.numeric, as.character) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -o_cat, names_to = c("Statistic", "heading"), names_sep = "__") %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = heading, values_from = value) %>% 
  select(-Statistic) %>% 
  add_row() %>%
  add_row(.before = 1) %>%
  add_row(.before = 1) %>%
  mutate(
    across(-1, ~case_when(row_number() == 1 ~ "%", TRUE ~ .x)), 
    across(1, ~case_when(row_number() == 2 ~ outcome_label, TRUE ~ .x)) 
  )
